description: GTFS source catalog for the gtfs feeder. Each entry describes one transit feed: GTFS-Realtime URL(s), optional GTFS Schedule URL(s), Mobility Database source ID, agency tag, route selector, and optional headers. Choose which entries run with the GTFS_SOURCES environment variable: a comma-separated list of names, or '*' for all; when unset, every entry whose 'enabled' flag is true is polled. This packaged catalog intentionally ships only disabled examples/templates because GTFS has no universal default feed. Point GTFS_SOURCES_FILE at a copy of this file to maintain your own catalog. Secrets may be written as ${ENV_VAR} and are expanded from the environment at load time, so keys never live in this file. The legacy GTFS_RT_URLS / GTFS_URLS / MDB_SOURCE_ID / AGENCY / ROUTE / GTFS_RT_HEADERS / GTFS_HEADERS inline configuration still works and takes precedence over this catalog when any legacy feed URL or MDB source ID is set.

sources.name: mbta-boston
sources.description: Massachusetts Bay Transportation Authority (Boston, US) public keyless GTFS-Realtime feeds and public GTFS Schedule archive.

sources.name: mta-nyc
sources.description: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (New York City, US) public keyless GTFS-Realtime feeds covering NYC Transit subway (all divisions: A/C/E, B/D/F/M, G, J/Z, L, N/Q/R/W, 1-7/S, Staten Island Railway), Long Island Rail Road, Metro-North Railroad, and system-wide service alerts, plus the public NYCT subway and borough-bus GTFS Schedule archives. No API key required since the 2023 MTA open-data migration to api-endpoint.mta.info. High volume: the combined feeds emit tens of thousands of trip-update, vehicle-position, and alert entities per poll. Add 'mta-nyc' to GTFS_SOURCES to enable.

sources.name: hsl-helsinki
sources.description: Helsingin seudun liikenne / Helsinki Regional Transport (HSL; Helsinki, Espoo, Vantaa, Kauniainen, Kerava, Kirkkonummi, Sipoo, Siuntio, Tuusula, and the surrounding capital region, Finland) public keyless GTFS-Realtime feeds served by the HSL GTFS-RT API (realtime.hsl.fi): vehicle positions (~1 s cadence), trip updates (~15 s), and service alerts (~5 min) for metro, tram, bus, commuter rail, and the Suomenlinna ferry, plus the public HSL GTFS Schedule archive. No API key is required for these protobuf feeds; the api.digitransit.fi subscription key applies only to the separate GraphQL routing/geocoding APIs, which this feeder does not use. Note: HSL trip IDs are unstable, so trips are identified by route_id + start_date + start_time + direction_id. Add 'hsl-helsinki' to GTFS_SOURCES to enable.

sources.name: bkk-budapest
sources.description: Budapesti Kozlekedesi Kozpont / Centre for Budapest Transport (BKK; Budapest, Hungary) GTFS-Realtime feeds from the BKK Futar OpenData API (go.bkk.hu): vehicle positions, trip updates, and service alerts for metro, tram, trolleybus, bus, the suburban railway (HEV), and Danube river boats. The realtime feeds require a free BKK OpenData key, supplied as the 'key' query parameter via the ${BKK_API_KEY} environment placeholder (register at opendata.bkk.hu, then generate a key under Key management). The public GTFS Schedule archive at bkk.hu is keyless. Export BKK_API_KEY and add 'bkk-budapest' to GTFS_SOURCES to enable.

sources.name: stm-montreal
sources.description: Societe de transport de Montreal (STM; Montreal, Quebec, Canada) GTFS-Realtime feeds from the STM Developpeurs API Hub (api.stm.info): vehicle positions and trip updates for the STM bus network and metro. The realtime feeds require a free STM API key, supplied as the 'apiKey' request header via the ${STM_API_KEY} environment placeholder (register at portail.developpeurs.stm.info, create an application, add the GTFS-Realtime (v2.0) API and accept its terms, then publish the application to activate the key). The public GTFS Schedule archive at stm.info is keyless. STM open data is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC-BY). Note: the STM GTFS-Realtime API exposes only vehicle positions and trip updates; there is no GTFS-Realtime service-alerts feed. Export STM_API_KEY and add 'stm-montreal' to GTFS_SOURCES to enable.

sources.name: sncf-france
sources.description: Societe nationale des chemins de fer francais (SNCF; France) national-rail GTFS-Realtime feeds published through the French National Access Point (transport.data.gouv.fr) proxy: trip updates and service alerts for the TGV / TGV INOUI high-speed network, Intercites, and TER regional express trains. The feeds are served keyless via proxy.transport.data.gouv.fr - the NAP re-publishes the SNCF feed with no API key required - and the SNCF GTFS Schedule archive on data.gouv.fr is likewise keyless. Note: this SNCF national-rail dataset exposes only trip updates and service alerts; there is no GTFS-Realtime vehicle-positions feed. SNCF open data is licensed under the Open Database License (ODbL). Add 'sncf-france' to GTFS_SOURCES to enable.

sources.name: tfnsw-sydney
sources.description: Transport for NSW (Sydney and New South Wales, Australia) API-key-protected GTFS-Realtime feeds for trains, buses, ferries, light rail, and service alerts. Add 'tfnsw-sydney' to GTFS_SOURCES to enable.

sources.name: keyed-operator-template
sources.description: TEMPLATE - copy this entry for an operator that requires an API key. Replace the URLs and agency tag with the operator's documented GTFS-Realtime and GTFS Schedule endpoints, export SOME_GTFS_KEY in the runtime environment, and set enabled to true.
